summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Facebook fraud becomes lightning rod
articles.description: Banks and consumer advocates are taking social media to task, while Facebook tries to rein in fake profiles and scam ads.

articles.title: Judge fines payments processor Cliq
articles.description: A federal court assessed a $6.5 million penalty over non-compliance in a 2015 Federal Trade Commission settlement but declined to appoint a receiver.

articles.title: CFPB alum Chopra to lead state consumer agency
articles.description: Former CFPB head Rohit Chopra will lead a new California agency with oversight of consumer affairs and financial protection, among other duties.

articles.title: The fight against fraud revs up
articles.description: Maybe crusaders against financial fraud and scams can finally make some headway with help from Congress, the White House and Nacha.

articles.title: Fiserv seeks better days ahead
articles.description: The payments processor and bank tech provider sought Thursday to bolster confidence in the company’s plan for a recovery.

articles.title: BNPL giants battle at Walmart
articles.description: The retail behemoth swapped buy now, pay later provider Affirm for Klarna last year, but both BNPL companies say Walmart business is good.

articles.title: Dive Deposits: Affirm’s AI stance runs counter to trend
articles.description: While some fintechs are content to do more with less through AI, BNPL platform Affirm simply aims to do more, as its top execs eschew human layoffs in favor of artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Block inches closer to DOJ settlement
articles.description: The company set aside $240 million in the first quarter as it negotiates with the Justice Department over allegations in a 2023 short seller’s report.
